# CLAUDE.md Pointer Patch

Idempotent append of a ≤50-line pointer block that directs any future Claude
Code session (or other AI agent reading `CLAUDE.md`) to the Boot Sequence
for the harness control loop.

Requirement refs: REQ-011, NFR-006, REQ-075.

## The block

Exactly this block is appended to the project's `CLAUDE.md`:

```markdown
## Harness (auto-generated by harness-init)

Before any action in this project:

1. Read `.harness/progress.md` (append-only worklog — tail is enough).
2. Read `.harness/_state.json` (current epic / sprint / phase / flags).
3. Follow `.claude/agents/planner.md` role assignment if participating in a
   sprint loop.

Enforcement level: **<hook_level>** (configured in `.harness/_config.yml`).
Unsafe operations are gated by `.harness/scripts/` hooks — do not bypass.

Do not edit `.harness/*` by hand except via the documented append protocol
(see `.harness/templates/shared_state.md`). Regenerate via `/harness-init`
if configuration needs to change.
```

`<hook_level>` is substituted from `_config.yml` at patch time
(`strict` / `warn` / `minimal`).

## Algorithm (idempotent)

```
marker := "## Harness (auto-generated by harness-init)"

if not exists CLAUDE.md:
    write CLAUDE.md with only the block
    return "created"

content := read CLAUDE.md
if marker in content:
    # Already patched. Check hook_level drift.
    if current block's hook_level != _config.yml.hook_level:
        replace the block in-place (from marker to next "## " or EOF)
        return "updated"
    return "skipped"

# Marker not present — append with a leading blank line.
append "\n" + block + "\n" to CLAUDE.md
return "appended"
```

The block is self-contained — no forward references to other documents that
may not exist yet. Length is always under 50 lines (NFR-006).

## Section boundaries

When updating in-place, the replacement ends at the first of:

- Next `## ` heading at the same level
- End of file

Deeper headings (`### `) inside the block are preserved as part of the block.

## Test recipe

```bash
# Case 1: no CLAUDE.md → created
rm -f CLAUDE.md
# run patch with hook_level=strict
grep -c "Harness (auto-generated" CLAUDE.md  # → 1

# Case 2: existing CLAUDE.md without marker → appended
echo "# My project" > CLAUDE.md
# run patch
grep -c "Harness (auto-generated" CLAUDE.md  # → 1

# Case 3: already patched, same hook_level → skipped (no change)
cp CLAUDE.md /tmp/before
# run patch again
diff /tmp/before CLAUDE.md  # → empty

# Case 4: hook_level changed → in-place replace
# change _config.yml hook_level to warn, re-run patch
grep "Enforcement level" CLAUDE.md  # → "warn"
```

## Non-goals

- Does not rewrite or reorder existing `CLAUDE.md` content.
- Does not de-duplicate unrelated sections.
- Does not touch `.claude/CLAUDE.md` (user-global) or subproject CLAUDE.md
  files — only the project-root `CLAUDE.md`.
